Biosphere Reserves — 0 features

Layer is empty by design. Rajasthan currently has no MoEFCC-notified Biosphere Reserve under the Man and Biosphere Programme. Reserved for a future notification.

Overlap analysis

Note: most containments below are expected by design — e.g., a National Park sits inside its Tiger Reserve; Wildlife Sanctuaries form buffer zones. They are listed for QA context, not as errors.

Recommendations before expanding

  1. Chase down point-only polygons — six environment features currently ship as points because their boundaries are not published in OpenStreetMap. Preferred sources: Rajasthan Forest Department shapefiles (formal request), NTCA notification annexures, or Ramsar RSIS site-shape uploads for Khichan and Menar.
  2. Confirm factual notes with a subject-matter reviewer. Every notes.facts entry is verifiable content; deeper source-linking is intentionally left as a follow-up.
  3. Add a name-in-Hindi field for Hindi-language readers. Schema supports properties.name:hi; source curriculum textbooks or Rajasthan gazette Hindi editions.
  4. Extend wetlands from the National Wetland Atlas (Space Applications Centre, ISRO). Current 8 major lakes are a starting set, not comprehensive.
  5. Do not open Module 3 (Rivers / Aravalli / Terrain) until this audit is reviewed. The plug-in path from ARCHITECTURE.md § "Adding a new layer" applies unchanged.
